displayName.text: SQUARE ENIX Cafe & Shop Los Angeles

text.text:
went to the squex cafe despite all the mid reviews - tbh the service was more disappointing than the food… we expected the food to be mid so we just ordered the Set C drink + dessert.
the drinks were great and desserts were 50/50. the yorha dessert is really good! the slime pancakes took a Reaaallly long time to come out and the butter was still hard 😭

the issue really was the front of house server, we arrived and she told us to wait outside without asking if we had a reservation? we said we had one and then she was like oh. and then let us sit in the waiting area. i totally understand being busy having worked in restaurants myself, but you should generally always ask “hi, do you have a reservation?” before anything else? it felt really off-putting.. so a star is taken away from how we were greeted, as well as how long the pancakes took. theres nothing in there for decoration which i really hope they add more, i would say any huge fans should go just for the instagram pics but avoid the entrees and expect mid and expensive 😭 the merch store staff were really nice!! they were also fans as well and we did end up buying the LA cafe shirts with the chocobo on it, its so cute!

text.text:
I heard the original opening didn't go super well but when I went this week during AX I thought it was pretty fun! The service was amazing, staff was really friendly and accommodating.

We ordered the white mage drink, hydaelyn drink, slime curry, moogle beef, minion dessert, nier dessert, and WoL cheesecake. The food was all very standard, honestly better than I expected for a themed cafe. Everything was edible. Out of the desserts my favorite was the minion flight and nier coffee jelly. But I really like creamy desserts.

originalText.text:

But I really like creamy desserts.

text.text:
I just went here today with some friends and can honestly say we enjoyed it! For a collab cafe, this was pretty good.

Food: I went in not really expecting it to be great, but was pleasantly surprised by what we saw/ate. I saw some photos posted by other reviewers of the slime curry and it looked watery compared to the promo pics. However, mine looked accurate to what was advertised (see attached). I have a feeling that because we went during a slow hour after a bit of the buzz died down, the food improved since Day 1? We really didn’t think it was as bad as other reviewers were saying (and we’re not die-hard SQE fans either lol.) At the end of the day, we came to take pictures of fun-looking food. ✨

Atmosphere: the interior could definitely use more decor. It feels a bit empty, I would love to see something similar to the Eorzea Cafes in Japan.

Service: The staff were incredibly kind, attentive, and quick with our orders! Thank you!

Overall, we concluded we’d like to come again, especially to check out the full menu when that’s ready! Don’t be deterred by other reviewers, and come for the cute food and drinks; I don’t want LA’s one good themed cafe to shut down!

text.text:
An exciting idea executed poorly.
I know my visit was only during the soft launch, but the experience was much more than just "teething problems".

Food:
VERY expensive considering the quality and portion size. Tasted like a reheated microwave meal and paid $25 for the pleasure. Drinks were... okay. Made to look good not taste good.

Service:
Staff were great and the only reason this review isn't 1*. Everyone who served us was friendly and attentive.

Atmosphere:
Non-existent. The room is white tables, bare white walls, like a lunch break canteen. No wall art, no props/photo-ops, just one glass case of merch in the corner and TV screens screaming "please buy our stuff and play our games!"

Whole thing feels like a cheaply executed way to sell merch. I feel like those who have rated this 5* are die hard fans who think Square Enix can do no wrong.
Come here if you want Squenix merch and quirky 'cocktails'. Go elsewhere for food.

text.text: To put it simply: Don’t come here. They didn’t theme the space at all; like if you removed all the purchasable things, this is just an empty white walled building. The food was way too expensive for way too basic, out pricing nearby theme parks’ dining options. The curry had just three pieces of chopped chicken and it was on the menu for $26. The cloud drink was just pineapple juice? Zero artistry in the visual of the space and the craft of the food. I’m sure it’ll be great for game events, they have a blank canvas. If it comes out that this place isn’t licensed at all, I wouldn’t be surprised. It was just nothing and bad food.
